nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸭科
nodes.summary: 鸭科（Anatidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联162种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、白背鸭、细斑树鸭等162种鸟类。

nodes.name: 埃及雁属
nodes.summary: 埃及雁属（Alopochen）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如埃及雁。

nodes.name: 埃及雁
nodes.summary: 埃及雁（Alopochen aegyptiaca）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如埃及雁。

nodes.name: 雁形目
nodes.summary: 雁形目（Anseriformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联166种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、角叫鴨、黑颈冠叫鸭等166种鸟类。

nodes.name: 尼罗河
nodes.summary: 尼罗河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括埃及雁、白眼潜鸭、日本鵪鶉等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 多瑙河
nodes.summary: 多瑙河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括埃及雁，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 撒哈拉以南非洲
nodes.summary: 撒哈拉以南非洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联90种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、埃及雁、Western crested guinea fowl等90种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 衣索比亞高原
nodes.summary: 衣索比亞高原是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联11种鸟类，包括埃及雁、黑冕鶴、肉垂鹤等11种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 埃及雁
nodes.summary: 埃及雁（Egyptian goose，学名 Alopochen aegyptiaca），分类上属于雁形目，并归入鸭科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点。
nodes.shortSummary: 埃及雁（学名：Alopochen aegyptiaca）为雁形目雁鸭科埃及雁属的唯一现生种，俗名秃雁。该物种的模式产地在埃及，廣泛分佈于埃及以及撒哈拉沙漠以南地區。 特征 埃及雁体重约1873克，翼长约356毫米，嘴峰长约51.6毫米，喙宽度约20.8毫米，喙厚度约16.7毫米…

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Egyptian goose is native to Sub-Saharan Africa and the Nile Valley, where it is widespread and common to abundant,{{cite book| last=Carboneras | first=C

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Egyptian goose is native to Sub-Saharan Africa and the Nile Valley, where it is widespread and common to abundant,{{cite book| last=Carboneras | first=C

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | date=30 September 2010 | title=Risk analysis of the Egyptian Goose in the Netherlands | url=https://www.buwa.nl/fileadmin/buwa_upload/Bureau_Waardenburg_rapporten/09-617_Egyptian_Goose_final_draft_30-09-2010_web.pdf | publisher=Bureau Waardenburg bv | access-date=10 July 2023 }} It is found in open or semi-open habitats, typically near fresh water, ranging from lowlands to above sea level in the Ethiopian Highlands, and largely absent from dense forests and deserts. While not breeding, it disperses somewhat, sometimes making longer migrations northwards into the arid regions of the Sahel, and occasionally even reaching the North African nations of Algeria and Tunisia, historically a more frequent part of its range. It was found in southeastern Europe (up to the lower Danube Valley and southern Hungary) until the early 1700s, and in parts of Turkey and the western Middle East until the early 1900s (and has since re-established through escaped captives), but its historical ranges in these places is incompletely known and the reason for the disappearances is also unknown.{{cite web| last=Marchant | first=J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | date=30 September 2010 | title=Risk analysis of the Egyptian Goose in the Netherlands | url=https://www.buwa.nl/fileadmin/buwa_upload/Bureau_Waardenburg_rapporten/09-617_Egyptian_Goose_final_draft_30-09-2010_web.pdf | publisher=Bureau Waardenburg bv | access-date=10 July 2023 }} It is found in open or semi-open habitats, typically near fresh water, ranging from lowlands to above sea level in the Ethiopian Highlands, and largely absent from dense forests and deserts. While not breeding, it disperses somewhat, sometimes making longer migrations northwards into the arid regions of the Sahel, and occasionally even reaching the North African nations of Algeria and Tunisia, historically a more frequent part of its range. It was found in southeastern Europe (up to the lower Danube Valley and southern Hungary) until the early 1700s, and in parts of Turkey and the western Middle East until the early 1900s (and has since re-established through escaped captives), but its historical ranges in these places is incompletely known and the reason for the disappearances is also unknown.{{cite web| last=Marchant | first=J
